response:
There are two popular products called **Comet**: an AI agentic browser (Perplexity’s Comet) and an ML observability/experiment tool (Comet ML). Below are cheaper alternatives for both; pick the section that matches what you meant.

---

## 1. Cheaper alternatives to **Perplexity Comet** (AI browsing / agent tool)

For “Comet” as the *Perplexity AI agent/browser*, these are lower‑cost or free alternatives:[2]

**Best free / very cheap options**

- **BrowserOS**  
  - **What it is:** Open‑source, privacy‑first Chromium‑based browser with local AI agents.[2]  
  - **Why cheaper:** The browser itself is **free**; you only pay model API costs if you connect cloud models.[2]  
  - **Good for:** Technical or privacy‑conscious users who like open source and local LLMs (via Ollama).[2]

- **GPT Breeze**  
  - **What it is:** Lightweight Chrome extension that adds AI shortcuts (summaries, rewrites, tab organization) into your current browser.[2]  
  - **Why cheaper:** Has a **free tier** with core features; paid plan only if you need more.[2]  
  - **Good for:** Casual productivity without switching browsers.

- **OpenAI Atlas (ChatGPT browser)**  
  - **What it is:** Browser experience integrated with ChatGPT, including an “Agent Mode.”[2]  
  - **Why cheaper:** The browser is free; Agent Mode comes with existing **ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) or Pro ($200/mo)** plans, so no separate Comet-like subscription if you already pay for ChatGPT.[2]  
  - **Good for:** Existing ChatGPT power users.

**Paid, still often cheaper / more focused**

- **eesel AI**  
  - **What it is:** AI agents that work inside your company tools (Zendesk, Slack, Confluence) for support and internal Q&A.[2]  
  - **Pricing:** Starts at **$299/month** for the Team plan; Business at $799/month.[2]  
  - **Good for:** Businesses wanting agents over internal docs rather than general web browsing.

- **Dia Browser**  
  - **What it is:** Minimalist AI browser with “Skills” (prebuilt prompt actions on pages).[2]  
  - **Pricing:** Cloud‑based, currently invite‑only beta; pricing information is more limited but positioned like a consumer browser product.[2]  
  - **Good for:** Users who care about design and simplicity.

If you tell me your budget and whether you want:  
- full AI browser,  
- a Chrome extension, or  
- an agent for internal company data,  
I can recommend one or two specific options.

---

## 2. Cheaper alternatives to **Comet ML** (experiment tracking / MLOps)

If you meant **Comet ML** (experiment tracking, model evaluation, GenAI observability), there are several cheaper or free options.[1]

**Open-source / free-first options**

- **MLflow**  
  - **What it is:** Open‑source platform for experiment tracking, model registry, and ML lifecycle management.[1]  
  - **Pricing:** **Free** as open source; you self‑host or run it yourself.[1]  
  - **When cheaper:** Ideal if you can manage infrastructure and want to avoid SaaS fees or lock‑in.

- **ZenML**  
  - **What it is:** Pipeline and lineage platform for ML and LLM evaluation workflows.[1]  
  - **Pricing:**  
    - Self‑hosted: **Free forever** (plus custom paid plans).[1]  
    - SaaS: From **$399/month**.[1]  
  - **When cheaper:** For teams willing to self‑host, this can be significantly cheaper than Comet SaaS.

- **Arize Phoenix**  
  - **What it is:** Open-source observability for generative AI, focusing on LLM tracing and evaluation.[1]  
  - **Pricing:** Open source (self‑host), so effectively free in license terms.[1]  
  - **When cheaper:** If your primary need is LLM tracing/eval rather than traditional ML.

- **Langfuse**  
  - **What it is:** Open-source LLM engineering platform for metrics, prompt management, and tracing.[1]  
  - **Pricing:** Open-source core; self‑hosting can be low‑cost compared with Comet.[1]  
  - **When cheaper:** LLM-heavy apps needing fine‑grained tracing/metrics.

**Low-cost SaaS tracking suites**

- **Weights & Biases (W&B + Weave)**  
  - **What it is:** Experiment tracking suite; Weave adds LLM tracing
